commit.message:
fix: dd on last empty line now deletes the trailing newline

line_range() returned an empty range when the cursor was on the
final empty line (start == end == len_chars). Vim deletes the
preceding newline in this case, removing the empty line and
moving the cursor up.

Fix: when the computed range is empty and we're not on line 0,
extend the range backward to include the previous line's trailing
newline.

commit.message:
[Repo Assist] Fix custom attribute encoding for generative type providers (#463)

🤖 *This PR was created by Repo Assist, an automated AI assistant.*

Two related fixes to custom attribute encoding in the generative type
provider assembly compiler (`AssemblyCompiler` / `defineCustomAttrs`).

## Fix 1: Implement array support in `encodeCustomAttrElemTypeForObject`

**Before:** Passing an `obj[]` (or typed array like `string[]`) as an
argument to an `Object`-typed custom attribute constructor parameter
would throw:
```
failwith "TODO: can't yet emit arrays in attrs"
```

**After:** Infers the element type from the runtime array type and
encodes `SZARRAY + element type byte` per ECMA-335 §II.23.3. Supported
element types: `string`, `bool`, `char`, `sbyte`/`byte`,
`int16`/`uint16`, `int32`/`uint32`, `int64`/`uint64`, `single`,
`double`, and `obj` (encoded as `OBJECT`).

## Fix 2: Apply `transValue` to all custom attribute argument values

**Before:** `transValue` (which translates `System.Type` → `ILType`) was
defined inside `defineCustomAttrs` but never used — the function was
dead code. Constructor arguments and named properties/fields were passed
through untranslated.

**After:** `transValue` is moved before its first use and applied to:
- All constructor fixed arguments (`constructorArgs`)
- Named property values (`namedProps`)
- Named field values (`namedFields`)

This ensures that if a custom attribute argument contains a
`System.Type` value (e.g., `typeof(SomeProvidedType)`), it is translated
to the target `ILType` and the correct type name is encoded in the
binary attribute blob.

## Root cause

Both issues affect **generative type providers** only. Erased providers
reflect attributes directly without going through ECMA-335 binary
encoding.

## Test Status

All existing tests pass: **104 passed, 0 failed** on net8.0.

```
Passed!  - Failed: 0, Passed: 104, Skipped: 0, Total: 104
````

Note: the new functionality (array args, Type args in attrs) is
exercised by the fixes themselves but no dedicated integration test is
added — adding one would require a new generative provider test fixture
with attributes using these specific parameter patterns.

commit.message:
cfl: CFL-075 EvaluateProfile iteration cap (timeout fix)

Cap nGran^ndim iterations in CIccEvalCompare::EvaluateProfile() to 100K.
A 6-channel profile with nGran=33 produces 33^6 = 1.29B iterations,
each calling Apply() twice — causing >1675s timeout.

Fix: dynamically reduce nGran so total iterations stay under 100K.
For ndim=6, nGran drops from 33 to ~6 (6^6=46656 iterations, ~3s).

This is a legitimate upstream algorithmic issue — iccRoundTrip also
hangs on this profile (verified via iccDEV/Build/Tools/).

CWE-400: Uncontrolled Resource Consumption

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix buffer stride calculation test and mark Phase 2.2 complete

Phase 2.2 (Buffer Allocator) was already implemented but had a failing test.
The test incorrectly expected X-tiled stride alignment to round up 7680 to
8192, when 7680 is already aligned to 512 bytes (7680 / 512 = 15 exactly).

- Added additional test cases for unaligned widths to verify alignment works
- All 24 Rust tests now pass
- All 20 Go test packages pass

Implementation complete:
✅ BufferAllocator: Allocates GPU buffers with X/Y-tiled formats (Intel i915/Xe)
✅ DMA-BUF export: export_dmabuf() provides fd for Wayland/X11 sharing
✅ SlabAllocator: Sub-allocation with first-fit strategy and coalescing

commit.message:
perf: wrap fingerprint batch writes in a transaction

set_fingerprints_batch() was executing 10K individual INSERT OR
REPLACE statements without a transaction — each auto-committed with
an fsync (~2ms on Windows). 10K fsyncs = ~23 seconds.

Wrap in a single transaction: 10K inserts with 1 fsync.

Previous: scan 709ms + read 148ms + write 23,766ms = 24.6s
Expected: scan 709ms + read 148ms + write ~700ms = ~1.6s

commit.message:
cfl: CFL-074 CheckUnderflowOverflow global ops budget (timeout fix)

Add global operation counter (100K budget) and reduce max recursion
depth from 100 to 16 in CheckUnderflowOverflow(). The original code
had O(nOps^depth) complexity — crafted calculator ops with nested
if/else/select could trigger >1000s execution in Begin().

Root cause: DoesStackUnderflowOverflow() calls CheckUnderflowOverflow()
twice (underflow+overflow check), each with depth-100 recursion but
no per-call operation budget. SequenceNeedTempReset() already had this
guard (kMaxOpsProcessed=1M) but CheckUnderflowOverflow() did not.

Fix: Add pOpsProcessed parameter (matches SequenceNeedTempReset pattern)
with 100K budget. Timeout PoC drops from 1011s to 37ms.

Verified: upstream iccDEV tools (iccDEV/Build/Tools/) handle the PoC
without issue (iccDumpProfile exits 0 in <1s).

CWE-400: Uncontrolled Resource Consumption

flush when the loop terminates naturally.

fix(concurrency): harden Orleans state, EF Core locks, WebSocket resilience

Orleans Grains State Safety:
- RiskManagementGrain: Added IPersistentState<RiskManagementState> with [GenerateSerializer],
  WriteStateAsync() after all mutations (positions, PnL, parameters, correlations)
- MLTrainingGrain: Added IPersistentState<MLTrainingState> with [GenerateSerializer],
  WriteStateAsync() for gradient buffer, training status, aggregated weights
- MarketRegimeGrain: Added IPersistentState<MarketRegimeState> with [GenerateSerializer],
  WriteStateAsync() for regime transitions, history, transition matrix
- DataIngestionGrain: Added IPersistentState<DataIngestionState> with [GenerateSerializer],
  WriteStateAsync() for channel metrics, tick counters (batched every 100 ticks)
- All State classes use [Id(N)] for Orleans serialization compatibility

DbContextWriteLock & EF Core Tracking:
- Verified all 10 repositories properly use DbContextWriteLock around SaveChangesAsync
- AssetPerformanceRepository confirmed protected (4 SaveChangesAsync calls)
- All repositories follow lock/try/finally/release pattern with ChangeTracker.Clear()

WebSocket Hardening:
- SendSubscriptionMessageAsync: Added try-catch for WebSocketException and
  OperationCanceledException around SendAsync (was previously unprotected)
- UnsubscribeFromMarketAsync: Added specific WebSocketException catch before
  generic Exception handler
- SubscribeToAccountAsync: Added specific WebSocketException catch before
  generic Exception handler
- ReceiveMessagesAsync: Catch all WebSocketException variants (not just
  ConnectionClosedPrematurely) to break receive loop on any WS error
- Added _consecutiveReceiveErrors counter for tight-loop protection
- Heartbeat/ping-pong already present via 30s KeepAliveInterval + 2min
  inactivity timeout

commit.message:
Replace Playwright with Naiad for pure .NET Mermaid rendering (#26)

* Replace Playwright with Naiad for pure .NET Mermaid rendering

- Replace Microsoft.Playwright dependency with Naiad (MermaidSharp) 0.1.2
  for pure .NET Mermaid-to-SVG rendering (no browser runtime required)
- Add Svg.Skia for high-quality SVG-to-PNG rasterization before Word embedding
- Add comprehensive SVG post-processing pipeline:
  - foreignObject-to-text conversion for Word compatibility
  - Modern color theme with Tailwind-inspired palette
  - Consistent styling across all diagram types (flowchart, sequence, class,
    state, ER, Gantt, pie) with unified borders, fonts, and colors
  - Diagram-specific normalizations (ER header overlays, class borders,
    Gantt task colors, pie chart series colors)
- Add light/dark theme system via --theme CLI parameter:
  - Page background, text colors, heading colors, code block styling
  - Theme-aware diagram rendering with distinct light/dark palettes
  - Dark syntax highlighting (VS Code dark theme colors)
- Add Consolas monospace font stack for all diagrams
- Diagrams always fill full page width with aspect ratio preservation
- Update tests for sync SVG API (91 tests passing)

Note: CI workflow changes (removing Playwright install steps) will be
submitted separately as the current token lacks workflow scope.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Drop netstandard2.1 target: Naiad only supports net10.0

Naiad 0.1.2 (pure .NET Mermaid renderer) only targets net10.0,
so netstandard2.1 multi-targeting is not possible. This removes:
- netstandard2.1 from TargetFrameworks (back to single TargetFramework)
- LangVersion 10.0 override (net10.0 defaults to C# 14)
- IsExternalInit polyfill (only needed for netstandard2.1)

All 99 tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove Playwright browser install steps from all CI workflows

Playwright is no longer used  Naiad provides pure .NET Mermaid
rendering. Removes the 'Ensure Playwright browsers are installed'
step from pr-check.yml, build-and-publish.yml, and dotnet-test.yml.

fix: improve token counting accuracy for context management (L3)

Replace naive len/4 estimation with a heuristic that accounts for
multi-byte characters (CJK/emoji), word boundaries, punctuation,
and per-message overhead. Estimates are typically within 20% of
actual BPE token counts. Shared implementation used by both
OpenAI and Ollama providers.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

perf: Global -O2 optimization + shader benchmark framework

- Set CONFIG_COMPILER_OPTIMIZATION_PERF=y in sdkconfig.defaults (global
  -O2 instead of -Og debug mode)
- Add -O2 -ffast-math to fw_tcp_server.c and fw_audio_output.c in
  CMakeLists.txt (render loop + audio ring buffer hot paths)
- Add esp32_firmware/scripts/shader_benchmark.py: automated benchmark
  that connects via telnet, runs all 20 native shaders, captures
  metrics, and writes a markdown report with build metadata
- Add esp32_firmware/SHADER_PERFORMANCE.md with current baseline

Key improvements vs -Og baseline:
  electric-arcs: 42.7ms → 19.6ms (2.2× faster, now 40 FPS)
  forest-wind:   35.8ms → 26.1ms (1.4× faster, 38.3 FPS)
  tone-pulse:    17.0ms → 3.8ms  (4.5× faster)

Global -O2 reduces I-cache pressure from system code on ESP32's 32KB
I-cache, significantly benefiting noise-heavy shaders. Audio dither
loop in fw_tcp_server.c benefits most from per-file -O2 -ffast-math.
